tldr: Moving from Algolia to Meilisearch trades a hosted proprietary search API for an open-source engine you can self-host or run on Meilisearch Cloud. Index schemas, ranking rules, and client integrations have to be re-mapped, but most apps can run both side-by-side during cutover.
why_switch: Lower and more predictable cost at scale
Open-source engine, self-host option removes vendor lock-in
Simple REST API and typo-tolerant relevance out of the box
Keep instant-search UX with comparable client SDKs
export_method: Use the Algolia dashboard or the official Algolia client SDK to export each index. The browse method (or equivalent in your SDK) lets you stream all records out as JSON, paginating until done. Pull index settings, synonyms, rules, and stop words separately through their respective endpoints in the SDK so nothing is lost. Keep one JSON file per index plus a settings file alongside it. Do not invent a one-click migration tool — script the export per index, verify counts against the dashboard, and store the dump in object storage so you can re-import if anything goes wrong. Capture API key definitions and scope restrictions from the dashboard for re-creation in the new system; secrets cannot be re-exported, only re-issued.
import_method: Spin up Meilisearch (Docker, self-hosted binary, or Meilisearch Cloud). Create one index per Algolia index, set the primary key, then bulk-add documents in batches using the Meilisearch client SDK or REST API. Translate Algolia settings into Meilisearch equivalents: searchableAttributes, filterableAttributes, sortableAttributes, ranking rules, synonyms, stop words. Re-issue API keys in Meilisearch with appropriate actions and index scopes — do not copy Algolia keys. Validate by replaying a sample of recent production queries against both engines and diffing the top results before flipping traffic. Run dual-write for at least one cycle so writes stay in sync until cutover.

concept_mapping.note: Order and semantics differ; review carefully

effort: 1-3 weeks for a typical app, longer if you rely heavily on Rules, Personalization, or Recommend
gotchas: Ranking semantics differ — relevance will not be byte-identical, plan for QA on top queries
Algolia Rules and Personalization have no 1:1 equivalent; budget for product-side rework
InstantSearch widgets need the Meilisearch InstantSearch adapter, not a drop-in swap
Plan capacity for self-hosted Meilisearch (RAM-bound) or factor in Meilisearch Cloud cost
Analytics history does not transfer; export anything you need for reporting first

email.subject: Cancellation and data export request for our Algolia account
email.body:
Hi Algolia team,

We are migrating off Algolia over the next 14 days and want to make sure the offboarding goes smoothly. Could you please confirm the cleanest way to export all of our indices, including documents, settings, synonyms, stop words, and rules, and let us know whether any of that requires support assistance versus what we can pull through the dashboard or API ourselves?

We would also like to schedule cancellation at the end of the current billing cycle once the export is verified. Please share the steps to disable auto-renewal, the date by which we need to confirm cancellation to avoid the next charge, and any data retention or deletion timelines after the account is closed.

If there is anything we should know about commit tiers or remaining credits before we cancel, please flag it. Aiming to wrap up within two weeks.

Thanks,
Your customer
